text.title: security-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for security-testing. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Run SAST (Semgrep, Bandit) and secret scanning (TruffleHog, Gitleaks) on every commit and every PR — these are fast (<2 min) and catch the majority of static vulnerabilities.
rule.rationale: Every commit is an attack surface change. Fast gates keep feedback immediate; deferred scanning gives vulnerabilities days to propagate.

rule.statement: Run SCA (Snyk, pip-audit, npm audit) on every build. Block on Critical CVEs or CVEs known to be exploited in the wild; warn on High CVEs with no fix available.
rule.rationale: Dependencies change on their own schedule. Every build is a new dependency risk surface. Critical exploitable CVEs are production emergencies.

rule.statement: Run DAST (OWASP ZAP, Nuclei) nightly on staging only — never against production or any environment with real user data or billing.
rule.rationale: DAST simulates attacks; running against production data causes real side effects. Staging must be isolated.

rule.statement: Run container scanning (Trivy) on every image build. Block on CRITICAL/HIGH severity unfixed vulnerabilities in the image.
rule.rationale: Container base images accumulate CVEs silently. Scanning at build time catches regressions before they reach the registry.

rule.statement: Define a baseline for pre-existing findings. Only new findings (relative to the baseline) block PRs. Pre-existing findings receive a triage decision once and move to an allowlist or backlog.
rule.rationale: Inheriting thousands of existing findings creates alert fatigue. New findings deserve immediate attention; legacy findings get tracked separately.

rule.statement: Any secret detected by TruffleHog or Gitleaks must block the PR with no exceptions. The secret must be revoked and rotated immediately — removal from git does not expire a leaked secret.
rule.rationale: Secrets pushed to git history are compromised forever. Rotation is the only mitigation; history scrubbing is secondary cleanup.

rule.statement: Add pre-commit hooks for Gitleaks and detect-secrets in every project. Developers should not be able to commit secrets accidentally.
rule.rationale: Pre-commit hooks catch the mistake before the git object exists, before the secret is ever visible in the remote.

rule.statement: Run threat modeling on new features before any tool touches them. Tools find known patterns; design flaws hide from scanners.
rule.rationale: BOLA, IDOR, and business logic abuse are the top API risk categories and are invisible to automated scanning.

rule.statement: SAST tools (Semgrep, Bandit, CodeQL, Snyk Code) analyze source code without executing the application. Run on every commit and PR.
rule.rationale: Static analysis is fast and catches the broadest range of code-level vulnerabilities. Multi-language tools like Semgrep enable polyglot coverage.

rule.statement: DAST tools (OWASP ZAP, Nuclei, Burp Suite, StackHawk) test running applications by simulating attacks. Use on staging environments only — never against production or any environment with real user data or billing.
rule.rationale: Dynamic testing finds runtime vulnerabilities and configuration issues that static analysis misses. Staging isolation prevents accidental data mutation.

rule.statement: Provide DAST tools with an authenticated session via a custom auth script — unauthenticated DAST misses 80% of the attack surface.
rule.rationale: Most application logic sits behind authentication. Unauthenticated scans find only public endpoints and misconfiguration.

rule.statement: SCA tools (Snyk, Trivy, pip-audit, npm audit, osv-scanner) scan dependencies for known CVEs and license issues. Run on every build.
rule.rationale: Third-party dependencies are attack vectors. Every build introduces new vulnerabilities from upstream releases.

rule.statement: Use reachability analysis (Snyk Code, Semgrep Pro) to distinguish exploitable CVEs from unreachable transitive dependencies. CVSS score alone is a poor triage signal.
rule.rationale: SCA generates high noise from transitive deps. A CVSS-10 in unreachable code is lower priority than a CVSS-6 in a public endpoint.

rule.statement: Secret detection tools (TruffleHog, Gitleaks, detect-secrets, GitHub Secret Scanning) find hardcoded credentials, API keys, and tokens in source code and git history. Run on every commit with pre-commit hooks.
rule.rationale: Secrets in git are compromised forever. Pre-commit detection prevents them from entering the repository in the first place.

rule.statement: For every authenticated endpoint, test: correct credentials (200), missing Authorization header (401), expired token (401), insufficient role (403), and another user's resource ID (403 or 404 — must not be 200).
rule.rationale: Horizontal privilege escalation (user A accessing user B's data) is the #1 API bug class and requires explicit per-endpoint testing.

rule.statement: BOLA (API1) and BOPLA (API3) require human review — scanners cannot detect them because the vulnerability is in business logic, not code patterns.
rule.rationale: Automated tools look for code signatures. BOLA exploits are valid HTTP requests using different IDs — indistinguishable from legitimate traffic to a scanner.

rule.statement: Define a triage process before connecting any scanner to CI. Pre-existing findings get one triage decision; only new findings block PRs.
rule.rationale: High false-positive rates from DAST and SCA erode trust in the pipeline. A triage process prevents "turn it off" reactions.

rule.statement: Sign artifacts (cosign/sigstore), pin base images by digest, generate SBOM (syft), and scan the SBOM (grype) as part of every container build.
rule.rationale: Supply chain attacks (OWASP A03 2025) target build artifacts and base images. Signing + SBOM provides provenance verification.

rule.statement: Human-in-loop on every Critical finding. Agents may auto-open PRs for Low/Medium findings with regression tests; Critical findings require human review before merge.
rule.rationale: The business and legal consequences of Critical vulnerabilities require human judgment about risk acceptance, timing, and disclosure.

rule.statement: Run penetration testing annually and after major architectural changes. Automated scanners do not replace pentesters for logic flaws and novel attack chains.
rule.rationale: Scanners find what they were programmed to find. Pentesters find what attackers find — including combinations of low-severity findings that chain into critical exploits.
